title: Cleaner
abstract: The present invention includes a first housing configured in a cylindrical shape, an inlet port provided to a circumferential surface of the first housing, a cyclone forming part provided within the first housing, a second housing coupled to an outer circumferential surface of the first housing and extending in a direction opposite to an extending direction of the inlet pipe, a fan provided within the second housing, a battery supplying power to the fan, a battery case coupled to the circumferential surface of the first housing and receiving the battery therein by extending in a direction opposite to the extending direction of the inlet pipe to be side by side with a length-directional axis of the second housing, and a handle coupled to a lateral surface of the battery case and a circumferential surface of the second housing.
claims: Claims ( 12 ) A cleaner, comprising: a first housing configured in a cylindrical shape; an inlet port provided to the first housing for air suction; a cyclone forming part provided within the first housing to separate dust from air flowing into the first housing; a second housing coupled to an outer circumferential surface of the first housing to communicate with the first housing and extending in a direction opposite to an extending direction of the inlet pipe; a fan provided within the second housing to provide a suction force so that air is sucked into the first housing through the inlet port; a battery supplying power to the fan; a battery case coupled to the circumferential surface of the first housing and receiving the battery therein by extending in a direction opposite to the extending direction of the inlet pipe to be side by side with a length-directional axis of the second housing; and a handle coupled to a lateral surface of the battery case and a circumferential surface of the second housing. The cleaner of claim 1, the handle comprising: a handle body extending from a bottom surface of the second housing in a length direction of the first housing; and an extending portion extending from one end of the handle body to be connected to the lateral surface of the battery case. The cleaner of claim 2, wherein the second housing is configured to have a height-directional length equal to or smaller than a half of a height-directional length of the first housing and connected to a top end of the circumferential surface of the first housing. The cleaner of claim 1, wherein the battery case and the handle body are spaced apart from each other on both sides with reference to a line passing through the length-directional axis of the inlet pipe. The cleaner of claim 3, wherein the extending portion is connected to a face confronting the handle body in the lateral surface of the battery case. The cleaner of claim 1, wherein the battery case and the second housing are located between both tangent planes of the first housing side by side with a face vertical to a ground surface by passing through the length-directional axis of the inlet pipe and the impeller rotation shaft. The cleaner of claim 1, further comprising a housing communicating hole formed in the circumferential surface of the first housing to enable the first and second housings to communicate with each other. The cleaner of claim 7, further comprising a second housing cover forming a rear surface of the second housing and having a multitude of air discharge holes, wherein the fan comprises a motor including a stator, a rotor rotated by the stator and an impeller rotation shaft provided vertical to a ground surface by having the rotor joined thereto and an impeller joined to the impeller rotation shaft. The cleaner of claim 1, wherein the battery case includes a battery insertion port formed by opening a rear surface of the battery case. The cleaner of claim 9, wherein the battery has a height-directional length greater than a front-rear length. The cleaner of claim 9, wherein the battery has a height-directional length smaller than a front-rear length. The cleaner of claim 9, wherein the battery case is configured to have a height-directional length greater than a front-rear length and wherein the battery comprises a battery frame receiving a battery cell therein and having a height-directional length smaller than a front-rear length and a cover portion extending upward from a rear surface of the battery frame to cover the battery insertion port.
